prompt: There's a nasty race in the attachment preview pane. A user opens an attachment, the preview finishes loading and renders — and if they hit Escape a beat later the pane blanks out and shows 'aborted': the data that was already on screen vanishes from the snapshot. QA has two more repros: the debug overlay showed three transitions for a single load (loading, aborted, then failed?!) when someone cancelled mid-download, and if you switch messages fast enough that the controller is already aborted before the preview starts, the loader still fires the network fetch. The contract we designed: each PreviewLoader runs one load; there is exactly one terminal transition; an abort must never undo a result that already settled; a pre-aborted signal never starts the fetch; and a transport that can't cancel must not be able to resurrect an aborted load with a late response. node --test test_preview_loader.ts reproduces every case. Fix preview_loader.ts and leave the tests alone.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: Build/import of preview_loader.ts exits 0. node --test exits 1 with 2 pass / 5 fail: a pre-aborted signal still invokes the fetcher, a mid-flight abort ends 'failed' with transitions [loading, aborted, failed], a late resolve from a non-cancellable transport flips an aborted load to 'loaded', an abort after a settled load blanks the loaded snapshot to {aborted, null, 'aborted'}, and a failed load is later overwritten to 'aborted'.
